question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The population gap between Montana and Michigan might exceed that between Michigan and Wisconsin.
options.B: Montana and Michigan have very similar populations.
options.C: All highlighted states have nearly identical populations.
options.D: The population differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Montana (12.7M) and Michigan (18.3M) have a 5.6M population difference, while Minnesota (1.6M) and Wyoming (2.1M) have only a 0.5M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Montana and Michigan into the same color bin (12-20M range), making their large difference appear similar, while Minnesota and Wyoming appear more different due to different color bins.
